svg.title: Full SD-JWT VC Structure — AV compliant
svg.text: Implemented in irmago Go code (solid border)
Spec feature — not implemented / not enforced (dashed)
Full SD-JWT VC Structure — AV compliant
IETF SD-JWT + SD-JWT VC · EUDI Age-Verification profile · as built in irmago/eudi/credentials/sdjwtvc
✓ tilde-separated · trailing ~ after each disclosure · KB-JWT appended only at presentation
signed once by the issuer at issuance
created by the holder, fresh per presentation
iss · vct · iat · exp · nbf · sub
the digests in _sd commit to the
Issuer-signed JWT payload — full claim set
holder public key, embedded at issuance
nested objects/arrays each carry their own _sd or "..." digests
only the disclosed claims' salts+values ever reach the verifier
✓ decoy digests can hide the real number of hidden claims
✗ status / revocation claim not yet enforced
sd_hash — binds KB-JWT to this presentation
SD-JWT VC §4.3 — recomputed at verify time
covers the issuer JWT + the disclosures actually
presented, up to & including the last ~,
any disclosure added, dropped or swapped
holder cannot present claims the issuer never signed
JWS signing input — the bytes ECDSA actually signs
RFC 7515 compact — used by BOTH the issuer JWT and the KB-JWT
verify = ECDSA-verify( pubKey, SHA-256(signing input), sig )
issuer JWT verified with the x5c DS public key (or did:web key)
KB-JWT verified with the holder public key taken from cnf
hashing the raw JSON payload (not its base64url form) → fails every time
most common JWS implementation mistake — mirrors the COSE Sig_structure trap
Who generates the holder key pair? (cnf)
✓ the HOLDER generates it locally — ideally inside Secure Enclave / TEE
✓ only the PUBLIC key is sent to the issuer, embedded in payload.cnf
✗ issuer NEVER sees the private key — would defeat key binding entirely
private key stays on device, signs a fresh KB-JWT for each presentation
public key locked into the issuer JWT once, signed by issuer, trusted thereafter
irmago holds holder private keys in KeyBindingStorage (batch-issued, one-time use)
exactly the TrustZone / StrongBox boundary as the mDoc device key
SD-JWT VC cnf ≙ mDoc MSO.deviceKeyInfo · KB-JWT ≙ mDoc deviceAuth
Two signatures — two ECDSA operations, two key pairs
proves: "claims are authentic, from a trusted issuer — and this holder key is bound"
proves: "this holder, right now, is presenting to THIS verifier for THIS request"
Both must pass — the issuer JWT + disclosures alone can be replayed by anyone who copied the credential.
The KB-JWT ties the presentation to a live session (nonce + aud) on the key-bound device.
a plain SD-JWT VC (no KB-JWT) is accepted only when key binding is not required by the verifier
Verification order (SD-JWT VC §7 — all steps required)
trust anchored in the x5c certificate chain (contrast: the mDoc learning test skipped chain validation)
Issuer JWT signature valid over the JWS signing input, using the DS public key from x5c (or did:web)
DS cert chains to a trusted root and is not revoked (CRL)
every presented disclosure's SHA-256 digest is found in _sd (recompute & match); no unreferenced disclosures
KB-JWT: signature valid via cnf key · sd_hash matches · nonce & aud match the request · iat fresh
⚠ KB-JWT verification currently supports cnf.jwk only — cnf.kid (did:jwk) resolution is a TODO
time checks: iat / nbf / exp within ± 180s clock skew
⚠ vct ↔ issuer-authorization check (requestor cert) is currently DISABLED (TODO) — an issuer's cert is not yet checked to be entitled to issue this vct.
⚠ credential status / revocation (status claim) is parsed but not yet enforced.
minimal disclosure — reveal a threshold, nothing more
vct identifies an age-verification credential type
each age_over_* is a selectively-disclosable boolean
the holder discloses ONLY the requested threshold
name, birth_date and every other claim stay withheld
undisclosed claims remain opaque digests in _sd — the verifier sees a hash, never a value
decoy digests can pad _sd so even the number of hidden claims is not revealed
SD-JWT VC ≙ mDoc: same AV goal, JWS/JSON instead of COSE/CBOR
Disclosure ≙ IssuerSignedItem · _sd digest ≙ MSO valueDigest · x5c ≙ DS cert in COSE hdr 33
Full SD-JWT VC Structure — solid = implemented in irmago | dashed orange = spec-only / not enforced | amber = crypto detail | green = trust & disclosure-digest links
